title: Strang's Optimization Core Concepts
description: A flowchart illustrating the fundamental optimization concepts in Gilbert Strang's approach to linear algebra, connecting matrices, subspaces, and optimization problems.
elements: Four Fundamental Subspaces (Row, Column, Null, Left Null)
relationships: Matrix A defines the Four Fundamental Subspaces
An inconsistent Ax=b leads to a Least Squares Problem
The Least Squares Problem is solved via the Normal Equation
The solution x̂ lies in the row space of A and provides the optimal projection of b onto the column space of A
